cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is about a study by the World Bank in 1974 regarding economic growth in some countries and the benefits to their populations. The options are from (A) to (D), each giving a different outcome of the study. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the text. The text says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the key point here is that the study found that the economic growth had "little benefit to a third" of their population. The other two-thirds would presumably have had some benefit, but the question is specifically about the study's conclusion regarding the benefit of economic growth. The question asks what the economic growth brought, and according to the passage, it was of little benefit to a third. This would mean that the majority (two-thirds) might have had some benefit, but the study is highlighting that a significant portion (a third) didn't. Now, looking at the options: (A) benefit to a third of their population: This would mean that the growth gave benefits to a third. But the passage says it was of little benefit to a third, implying that the rest (two-thirds) did benefit, but the focus of the study is on the problem that a third didn't get the benefit.
